perturbation.original_text: 1. "Confidential Information" means non-public information relating to the disclosing party's business that the disclosing party designates as being confidential or proprietary or which, under the circumstances surrounding disclosure, ought to be treated as confidential. "Confidential Information" includes, without limitation, ideas, concepts, designs, specifications, drawings, blueprints, tracings, models, samples, data, computer programs, marketing plans and strategies, business strategies, customer names, mailing lists, prices, rates, costs, information received from others that the disclosing party is obligated to treat as confidential, and other technical, financial or business information.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. "Confidential Information" means non-public information relating to the disclosing party's business that the disclosing party designates as being confidential or proprietary or which, under the circumstances surrounding disclosure, ought to be treated as confidential. "Confidential Information" includes, without limitation, ideas, concepts, designs, specifications, drawings, blueprints, tracings, models, samples, data, computer programs, marketing plans and strategies, business strategies, customer names, mailing lists, prices, rates, costs, information received from others that the disclosing party is obligated to treat as confidential, and other technical, financial or business information. **However, this definition excludes any information that is independently developed by the receiving party, regardless of its similarity to the disclosing party's information.**
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 1 adds a clause that excludes independently developed information from the definition of 'Confidential Information'. This directly contradicts the obligations of the receiving party outlined in Section 2, which requires them to protect Confidential Information, even if they independently develop similar information. This creates uncertainty as to whether independently developed information, similar to the disclosing party's, needs to be protected, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Obligations of Receiving Party. The receiving party will protect Confidential Information by using at least the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of such Confidential Information as the receiving party uses to protect its own confidential or proprietary information. The receiving party will neither disclose nor copy Confidential Disclosure except as necessary for its employees, agents or representatives with a need to know, provided that any such employee, agent or representative shall have agreed in writing, as a condition to his or her employment or engagement or in order to obtain Confidential Information, to be bound by non-disclosure and non-use obligations substantially similar to this Agreement. Any copies which are made will be identified as belonging to the disclosing party and shall be reproduced with the disclosing party's proprietary rights notices in the same manner in which such notices appear in the original copy provided by the disclosing party. The receiving party will not use the Confidential Information for any purpose except to evaluate a business relationship with the disclosing party or otherwise in pursuance of such a relationship. Nothing in this Agreement will be construed to limit the receiving party's right to independently develop information similar to the Confidential Information or to compete with the disclosing party so long as the receiving party does not violate its obligations hereunder regarding the non-disclosure and use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the definition of 'Confidential Information', excluding independently developed information. The contradicted text, Section 2, states the receiving party must protect Confidential Information, and nothing should limit the receiving party's right to independently develop information similar to Confidential Information as long as they don't violate obligations regarding non-disclosure and use of Confidential Information. Because independently developed information can now be excluded from protection per the changed text, this creates a direct conflict with the original obligation in the contradicted text to protect it as Confidential Information, regardless of independent development, thereby creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Ownership of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information shall remain the property of the disclosing party. By disclosing information to the receiving party, the disclosing party does not grant any express or implied right to the receiving party to or under any of the disclosing party's patents, copyrights, trademarks or trade secret information. The receiving party will return all originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confidential Information immediately upon the disclosing party's request or, at the disclosing party's option, destroy the same, and will deliver to the disclosing party, upon its request, a certificate of a duly authorized officer certifying as to the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Ownership of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information shall remain the property of the disclosing party, **except for marketing plans which are now considered joint property.** By disclosing information to the receiving party, the disclosing party does not grant any express or implied right to the receiving party to or under any of the disclosing party's patents, copyrights, trademarks or trade secret information. The receiving party will return all originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confidential Information immediately upon the disclosing party's request or, at the disclosing party's option, destroy the same, and will deliver to the disclosing party, upon its request, a certificate of a duly authorized officer certifying as to the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except for marketing plans which are now considered joint property' to Section 3, ownership of marketing plans is now ambiguous. This contradicts the requirement that the receiving party return 'all' Confidential Information. If marketing plans are jointly owned, the receiving party might argue they are not obligated to return them. This creates an in-text contradiction concerning the obligation to return all confidential information, particularly marketing plans.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Ownership of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information shall remain the property of the disclosing party, except for marketing plans which are now considered joint property. By disclosing information to the receiving party, the disclosing party does not grant any express or implied right to the receiving party to or under any of the disclosing party's patents, copyrights, trademarks or trade secret information. The receiving party will return all originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confidential Information immediately upon the disclosing party's request or, at the disclosing party's option, destroy the same, and will deliver to the disclosing party, upon its request, a certificate of a duly authorized officer certifying as to the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to Section 3, stating that 'marketing plans are now considered joint property,' while the contradicted text is the entirety of Section 3, which includes the original stipulation that 'all Confidential Information shall remain the property of the disclosing party' and that 'the receiving party will return all originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confidential Information.' This creates a direct contradiction because jointly owned marketing plans would not necessarily be subject to the return clause, thus the Change Text directly contradicts the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Remedies. The receiving party acknowledges that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement, and the disclosing party will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies will not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but will be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Remedies. The receiving party acknowledges that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement, and the disclosing party will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. **However, the disclosing party's right to injunctive relief is limited to cases where monetary damages are demonstrably impossible to calculate.** Such remedies will not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but will be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: Section 6 originally states that the disclosing party is entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief for any breach. The added clause limits the right to injunctive relief, contradicting the initial statement that such relief is available for any breach. Now, injunctive relief is only available when monetary damages are impossible to calculate creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Remedies. The receiving party acknowledges that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement, and the disclosing party will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. However, the disclosing party's right to injunctive relief is limited to cases where monetary damages are demonstrably impossible to calculate. Such remedies will not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but will be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the disclosing party is entitled to injunctive relief for *any* breach. The changed text limits this right, stating injunctive relief is only available when monetary damages are demonstrably impossible to calculate. This directly contradicts the original statement and creates an in-text contradiction as it's in the same location. The changed text alters the scope of the entitlement breaking the previous consistent meaning.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Term. The obligations of each receiving party hereunder with respect to the non-disclosure and use of the disclosing party's Confidential Information shall survive until such time as all such Confidential Information disclosed hereunder becomes publicly known and made generally available through no action or inaction of the receiving party.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Term. The obligations of each receiving party hereunder with respect to the non-disclosure and use of the disclosing party's Confidential Information shall survive until such time as all such Confidential Information disclosed hereunder becomes publicly known and made generally available through no action or inaction of the receiving party. **Notwithstanding the foregoing, the obligations regarding customer lists shall expire after one year.**
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence contradicts the term's clause stated in Section 8. The original text states that all Confidential Information obligations last until the information is publicly known. However, the added text specifies that obligations regarding customer lists expire after one year, regardless of whether the customer lists become public. This presents an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Term. The obligations of each receiving party hereunder with respect to the non-disclosure and use of the disclosing party's Confidential Information shall survive until such time as all such Confidential Information disclosed hereunder becomes publicly known and made generally available through no action or inaction of the receiving party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the obligations regarding customer lists shall expire after one year.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence stating that obligations regarding customer lists expire after one year, contradicting the original text's statement that confidentiality obligations last until the information is publicly known. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which doesn't change the fact that a contradiction was introduced. This creates a direct conflict within Section 8 regarding the duration of confidentiality, and thus a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 9. General. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior or contemporaneous agreements or understandings, written or oral, concerning the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be amended, modified or revoked only by a written instrument executed by all parties hereto. Failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver of any term hereof, and no waiver of a provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any other provision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective heirs, legal representatives, successors and assigns. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Minnesota. Each party consents to personal jurisdiction in any action brought in any court, federal or state, of competent jurisdiction within the State of Minnesota. If any provision of this Agreement shall be held by a court of competent jurisdiction to be illegal, invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. General. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior or contemporaneous agreements or understandings, written or oral, concerning the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be amended, modified or revoked only by a written instrument executed by all parties hereto. **However, either party may unilaterally amend this agreement with 30 days written notice.** Failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver of any term hereof, and no waiver of a provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any other provision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective heirs, legal representatives, successors and assigns. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Minnesota. Each party consents to personal jurisdiction in any action brought in any court, federal or state, of competent jurisdiction within the State of Minnesota. If any provision of this Agreement shall be held by a court of competent jurisdiction to be illegal, invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 9 states that the agreement can only be amended by a written instrument executed by all parties. The added sentence allows unilateral amendment with 30 days' notice. This creates an in-text contradiction as to how the agreement can be modified, potentially leading to disputes about the validity of any unilateral changes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. General. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior or contemporaneous agreements or understandings, written or oral, concerning the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be amended, modified or revoked only by a written instrument executed by all parties hereto. However, either party may unilaterally amend this agreement with 30 days written notice. Failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver of any term hereof, and no waiver of a provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any other provision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective heirs, legal representatives, successors and assigns. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Minnesota. Each party consents to personal jurisdiction in any action brought in any court, federal or state, of competent jurisdiction within the State of Minnesota. If any provision of this Agreement shall be held by a court of competent jurisdiction to be illegal, invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence stating "However, either party may unilaterally amend this agreement with 30 days written notice." This directly contradicts the preceding sentence in the same section, which states, "This Agreement may be amended, modified or revoked only by a written instrument executed by all parties hereto." This introduces a clear in-text contradiction regarding how the agreement can be amended, fitting the definition of a structural flaw creating an in-text contradiction.
